study: SG-WF-004 taddhita ‚ÄĒ hand-adjudication of the seeded surface-suffix sample
sample_kind: freq-weighted draw of distinct nominal (NOUN/ADJ) lemma types matching a canonical taddhita suffix (estimates token-mass composition)

categories.taddhita: genuine secondary derivation from a nominal base (devatńĀ‚Üźdeva, vńęrya‚Üźvńęra, pakŠĻ£in‚ÜźpakŠĻ£a, guŠĻáavat‚ÜźguŠĻáa, gomaya‚Üźgo, ńĀditya‚Üźaditi)
categories.krt: primary/deverbal ‚ÄĒ the -ya/-aka/-in ending is a kŠĻõt gerundive/agent on a verbal root (kńĀrya‚ÜźkŠĻõ, ramaŠĻáńęya‚Üźram, vyńĀpaka‚ÜźvyńĀp, kńĀŠĻÖkŠĻ£in‚ÜźkńĀŠĻÖkŠĻ£), NOT a taddhita suffix
categories.primary: underived primary noun/adjective whose ending merely resembles a suffix (sŇęrya, haya, hiraŠĻáya, priya, cintńĀ, maya, niŇõcaya)
categories.pronoun_name: lemmatized pronoun form or proper name (tńĀ = nom/acc pl of tad)
categories.compound: the ending belongs to a compound final member, not a suffix on this stem (evaŠĻÉvńęrya)

tally.note: surface taddhita-suffix matching over the nominal lexicon is ~50 % precise; the other ~50 % of the token mass is kŠĻõt gerundives/agents (-ya/-aka/-in on verbal roots, 27 %), primary nouns (20 %), pronoun forms and compound finals. Comparable to the kŠĻõt-name pilot P5 (~41 % precision / ~59 % false). Freq-weighted sample ‚Üí estimates token-mass composition.

worst_contaminant.why: the largest surface bucket by far; conflates taddhita relational/abstract (vńęrya, indriya, sainya) with kŠĻõt gerundives (kńĀrya, ŇõiŠĻ£ya, martya) and primary nouns (sŇęrya, haya, hiraŠĻáya, priya)
